---
title: "AS 209 — Protoplanetary Disk"
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
    css: styles.css
    code-fold: false
lightbox: true
---

![ALMA image of the protoplanetary disk around AS 209 showing multiple narrow rings and gaps that may indicate ongoing planet formation. Credit: ALMA (ESO/NAOJ/NRAO); A. Sierra (U. Chile).](images/as_209_ficha.png){#fig-as209 fig-align="center" width="650"}

As shown in @fig-as209, the AS 209 disk exhibits a sequence of bright rings and dark gaps in the dust continuum emission.

## Object names

| Catalog | Name |
|---|---|
| Primary name | AS 209 |
| Other designations | — |
| Region | Ophiuchus star-forming region |

## General system properties

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Distance | 126 pc |
| Age | ~0.5–1 Myr |
| Spectral type | K5 |
| Stellar temperature | 4250 K |
| Stellar mass | ~0.9 M☉ |
| Stellar luminosity | ~1.5 L☉ |
| Disk radius | ~200 AU |
| Disk inclination | ~35° |

## Observations

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Instrument | ALMA |
| ALMA band | Band 6 |
| Observing frequency | ~230 GHz |
| Wavelength | 1.3 mm dust continuum |
| Observation date | 22–26 September 2016 |
| Number of antennas | 38–41 |
| Integration time | ~40 min per execution block |
| System temperature | 80–145 K |
| Precipitable water vapour | 2.2–2.3 mm |
| Peak flux | 13 mJy beam⁻¹ |
| RMS noise | 0.1 mJy beam⁻¹ |

## Disk geometry

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Disk inclination | 35.3° |
| Position angle | 86° |
| Synthesized beam | 0.19″ × 0.14″ |

## Disk morphology

ALMA observations reveal a structured disk with multiple rings and gaps in the millimeter dust continuum emission. Two prominent rings are located at approximately **75 AU** and **130 AU**, separated by two narrow gaps at **62 AU** and **103 AU**.

## Morphology measurements

| Structure | Type | Radius (AU) |
|---|---|---|
| G1 | Dark gap | 62 |
| R1 | Bright ring | 75 |
| G2 | Dark gap | 103 |
| R2 | Bright ring | 130 |

## Gap properties

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Gap 1 half width | ~8 AU |
| Gap 2 half width | ~16 AU |

## Disk size

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Outer dust emission | ~170–180 AU |

## Evidence of planet formation

Hydrodynamic simulations of planet–disk interactions suggest that the observed gaps could be produced by embedded planets. The outer gap is consistent with a planet of approximately **0.7 Saturn masses**, while the inner gap may be produced by a lower-mass planet with a mass below **0.1 Jupiter masses**. The radial positions of the two gaps are also close to the ratio expected for planets trapped in a **2:1 orbital resonance**, a configuration often predicted in planet formation models.

## Activity

**Coming soon**

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
